mappings.rationaleAI: Film as a visual art form encompassing fiction, documentary, animation, and experimental modes addresses the nature and classification of motion pictures (A.1), component arts including script, acting, directing, editing, and technology (A.2), and production processes (A.3).
Entertainment as mass-audience activities connect to the motion-picture industry (C.1).

Disney's pioneering of synchronized sound and full-color Technicolor animation addresses technology in the creative process including animation (A.2.e), while his expansion into theme parks and media connects to the motion-picture industry (C.1).
Animation as a filmmaking technique using hand-drawn cels, CGI, and stop motion directly addresses the use of technology including animation and special effects (A.2.e).

Theatre's acting techniques and live performance address motion-picture acting and its distinction from theatre (A.2.b), while theatrical arts broadly connect to the interrelation of other arts in motion pictures (B).
Literature's dramatic and narrative forms connect to the writer of the script or screenplay (A.2.a) and the interrelation of literature in motion pictures (B).

Chaplin's total artistic control as writer, director, producer, editor, and composer addresses acting (A.2.b) and the role of the director (A.2.c), while his political satire connects to motion pictures as media of education and propaganda (C.2).
Photography's capture of images through optical and sensor technology connects to the use of the camera in the creative process (A.2.e).
Television as a broadcast medium connects to the motion-picture industry and its distribution channels (C.1).
The camera as an instrument capturing images through lens, aperture, and shutter mechanisms directly addresses the use of the camera in the creative process (A.2.e).
Edison's contributions to motion picture technology and the phonograph address the use of technology in the creative process (A.2.e).
Popular culture's film, music, and entertainment industry connect to the motion-picture industry (C.1).
Modernism's cinematic montage and experimental techniques address the nature and classification of motion pictures (A.1).
Dance as an art form performed in motion pictures connects to the interrelation of dance in motion pictures (B).
Music's role in film scoring and soundtracks connects to the interrelation of music in motion pictures (B).
Hitler's use of mass media propaganda and the Nazi film industry's deployment of cinema for ideological purposes connect to the use of motion pictures as media of propaganda (C.2).
